data.body:
## Description

When merge-to-main.sh runs the version_bump phase, bump-version.sh modifies plugin.json and stages it. If the subsequent git commit fails (e.g., pre-commit test gate blocks because record-test-status.sh was not run), the staged change to plugin.json is left dirty in main's working tree. On --resume, the script re-runs bump-version.sh (double-bumping), and the merge retry fails with 'Your local changes would be overwritten by merge'.

## Root Cause

The version_bump phase in merge-to-main.sh does not:
1. Run record-test-status.sh before committing the version bump
2. Run record-review.sh before committing the version bump
3. Clean up the staged file on commit failure (no rollback)

## Expected Behavior

If the version bump commit fails for any reason, merge-to-main.sh should restore main's working tree to a clean state (git checkout -- the bumped file) before exiting or retrying. The --resume path should detect an already-bumped file and skip re-bumping.

## Reproduction

1. Run merge-to-main.sh --bump minor on a worktree with commits
2. Observe: bump-version.sh stages plugin.json, commit fails at test gate
3. Run merge-to-main.sh --resume
4. Observe: double bump + merge failure due to dirty working tree
